workflow_name: Internal Account Review Transcript -> Team Actions
user_message: Just came out of security posture with Contoso Ltd. Turn this into my brief and pull out the asks.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: security posture Discussion - Contoso Ltd
Date: 2024-03-08
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Angela Doyle (Contoso Ltd), Marcus Williams (Microsoft), Priya (Contoso Ltd)

[00:01:15] Angela Doyle (Contoso Ltd): Appreciate everyone making time. The main agenda today is security posture.

[00:06:00] Angela Doyle (Contoso Ltd): The timeline for partner engagement is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:10:00] Priya (Contoso Ltd): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about budget approval. Can we address those?

[00:12:00] Priya (Contoso Ltd): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about data residency. Can we address those?

[00:14:00] Angela Doyle (Contoso Ltd): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is vendor dependencies.

[00:16:00] Angela Doyle (Contoso Ltd): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about vendor dependencies. Can we address those?

[00:21:00] Angela Doyle (Contoso Ltd): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about stakeholder alignment. Can we address those?

[00:25:00] Priya (Contoso Ltd): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is performance requirements.

[00:27:00] Angela Doyle (Contoso Ltd): The timeline for data residency is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

handoffs.condition: team coaching or risk review input

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF52-N trap (Separation of customer-facing draft from internal brief when one unified output ); produces complete Team action items with owners, coaching notes (constructive frame), customer-facing draft (sanitized
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF52-N failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF52-N failure mode (Separation of customer-facing draft from internal brief when one unified output )
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct security posture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF52-N -- Separation of customer-facing draft from internal brief when one unified output
Guardrail axis scored neutrally -- focus on whether output served the user's actual need
